name: sol_tracking
description: a statute-of-limitations tracking world per claim; actions accrue, toll, file, bar

card.description: a statute-of-limitations tracking world per claim; actions accrue, toll, file, bar

rules: A claim starts 'pending'. 'accrue' starts the limitations clock once, recording the accrual day and moving the claim to 'running'.
'tick' advances the calendar one day (or params['days']). A 'running' clock adds one elapsed day toward the limit per day; a 'tolled' clock adds a toll day instead and does not approach the limit.
'toll' pauses a 'running' clock for a stated reason (e.g. minority, defendant absence, fraudulent concealment); 'resume' restarts it.
elapsed_days never decreases; remaining_days = max(0, period - elapsed).
'file' succeeds only for an accrued claim that is unfiled, unbarred, and still within the period; it stops the clock and satisfies the SOL.

When elapsed_days reaches the limitations period on an unfiled claim, the claim becomes time-barred — automatically on 'tick', or on demand via 'bar' (the limitations defense). 'filed' and 'barred' are terminal.

transition.code:
def transition(state, action):
    # Statute-of-limitations clock for a single claim. The limit is counted in
    # *non-tolled* elapsed days since accrual; tolling pauses that count while the
    # calendar keeps moving. Filing satisfies the SOL; running out bars the claim.
    s = dict(state)
    name = action.get("name")
    params = action.get("params") or {}
    period = s["limitations_period_days"]

    if name == "accrue":
        # The cause of action accrues: the limitations clock starts. Idempotent —
        # a claim accrues exactly once, only out of the 'pending' status.
        if s["status"] == "pending" and not s["accrued"]:
            s["accrued"] = True
            s["accrual_day"] = s["day"]
            s["elapsed_days"] = 0
            s["tolled"] = False
            s["status"] = "running"
            s["remaining_days"] = period

    elif name == "toll":
        # Pause the running clock for a legal tolling reason. Counted days freeze.
        if s["status"] == "running":
            s["tolled"] = True
            s["status"] = "tolled"
            s["toll_reason"] = str(params.get("reason", "unspecified"))

    elif name == "resume":
        # The tolling condition is lifted; the clock runs again.
        if s["status"] == "tolled":
            s["tolled"] = False
            s["status"] = "running"
            s["toll_reason"] = ""

    elif name == "tick":
        # Advance the calendar by one day (or params['days']). Only a *running*
        # clock accrues elapsed days toward the limit; a tolled clock banks
        # toll_days instead; terminal/pending claims just see the date move.
        days = params.get("days", 1)
        if not isinstance(days, int) or isinstance(days, bool) or days < 0:
            days = 1
        for _ in range(days):
            s["day"] += 1
            if s["status"] == "running":
                s["elapsed_days"] += 1
                if s["elapsed_days"] >= period:
                    # Period exhausted with no filing -> time-barred.
                    s["status"] = "barred"
                    s["barred"] = True
            elif s["status"] == "tolled":
                s["toll_days"] += 1
        s["remaining_days"] = max(0, period - s["elapsed_days"])

    elif name == "file":
        # File suit. Succeeds only if the claim has accrued, is not already filed
        # or barred, and the clock still has time left. Filing stops the clock.
        if (s["accrued"] and not s["barred"] and not s["filed"]
                and s["elapsed_days"] < period):
            s["filed"] = True
            s["filing_day"] = s["day"]
            s["status"] = "filed"
            s["remaining_days"] = max(0, period - s["elapsed_days"])

    elif name == "bar":
        # Raise the limitations defense: if the clock has run out on an accrued,
        # unfiled claim, it is time-barred.
        if (s["accrued"] and not s["filed"] and not s["barred"]
                and s["elapsed_days"] >= period):
            s["status"] = "barred"
            s["barred"] = True
        s["remaining_days"] = max(0, period - s["elapsed_days"])

    return s

emit.report: SOL claim status: {status} (day {day}). Accrued day {accrual_day}; {elapsed_days}/{limitations_period_days} days elapsed, {remaining_days} remaining. Tolled={tolled} ({toll_reason}). Filed={filed} (day {filing_day}). Time-barred={barred}.

objectives.name: timely_filing
objectives.goal: file the claim before it is time-barred
